prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: One writer I believe is under-discussed is Katherine Mansfield. Despite being a key figure in the modernist short story movement, her work is often overshadowed by that of contemporaries like Virginia Woolf and James Joyce. Mansfield's writing was groundbreaking in its psychological insight, emotional complexity, and experimental style. Her ability to convey the inner lives of her characters, often women constrained by societal expectations, was years ahead of her time. More attention should be paid to her highly influential body of work.
